title: Today's AI Brief: MUFG aims to become AI-native with OpenAI
summary: MUFG partners with OpenAI, automakers stockpile vehicles amid supply chain issues

body.text: I've been catching up on some interesting developments in the world of AI. MUFG, a major financial institution, has announced plans to become AI-native with the help of OpenAI. This means they'll be using ChatGPT Enterprise to streamline workflows and create new AI-powered financial services. It's a big deal because it shows how AI can be used to transform traditional industries. I wonder what this means for the future of banking - will we see more AI-driven financial services?

MUFG's partnership with OpenAI is all about leveraging AI to improve efficiency and deliver new services. By using ChatGPT Enterprise, they'll be able to automate tasks, gain insights from data, and create more personalized experiences for customers. What's interesting here is that MUFG is taking a proactive approach to AI adoption, rather than just reacting to it. I've heard that ChatGPT Enterprise can handle a wide range of tasks, from data analysis to content generation - it's exciting to think about the possibilities.

Meanwhile, automakers are stockpiling vehicles due to supply chain disruptions caused by the war. It's a classic case of 'just in case' inventory management. I mean, who doesn't love a good game of automotive Jenga? But seriously, this highlights the challenges that companies face when global events impact their operations. And it's not just automakers - many industries are feeling the pinch of supply chain disruptions.

My quick read: the artificial intelligence story keeps getting bigger, but also stranger in very human ways. One moment it is strategy and infrastructure; the next, the Vatican is in the room. That is a pretty good sign this technology has left the lab and wandered into the town square.

sources.title: MUFG aims to become AI-native with OpenAI
Automakers stockpile vehicles amid war-shaken supply chains - Axios
